metadata.title: Insertion of customised titanium implants, with soft tissue cover, for orofacial reconstruction
specialty_societies: British Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons
Craniofacial Society of Great Britain and Ireland
metadata.scope_boundary: Implants WITH soft tissue cover or expected to become covered. Does NOT cover complex reconstruction with multiple bony/cartilaginous structures with little/no soft tissue cover (see HTG313).
metadata.related_guidance: HTG313 (exposed implants without soft tissue cover)

nodes.label: Customised titanium implant insertion with soft tissue cover for orofacial reconstruction

properties.key_distinction: Implant must be covered or expected to become substantially covered with soft tissue
properties.what_is_customised: Implant shape pre-designed from patient CT data using CAD-CAM, rather than bent intraoperatively
properties.coverage: existing local soft tissue or transplanted flap from donor site
properties.exception_to_coverage: Implants abutting paranasal sinuses may be partially exposed - epithelialisation expected via fibrosis
properties.computer_navigation_option: Intraoperative computer-assisted navigation used in some cases to verify implant positioning
properties.tacit: The critical clinical decision is whether adequate soft tissue coverage can be achieved or expected. If coverage is uncertain or impossible, this procedure is out of scope; the clinical and governance pathway changes to HTG313.

properties.method: Titanium mesh bent and cut during operation to fit defect
properties.limitation: Multiple insertion attempts may be needed to achieve correct shape
properties.consequence_of_multiple_attempts: Increased soft tissue manipulation, longer operating time, greater trauma
properties.tacit: The key disadvantage is iterative intraoperative adjustment; each re-attempt increases operating time, soft tissue trauma, and risk of contamination or structural damage

properties.type: Patient's own bone harvested from donor site
properties.traditional_use: Historical standard for large orbital and facial fracture repair
limitations: Variable resorption rate - clinical outcomes may be unpredictable
properties.tacit: Variable resorption is the key limitation - a graft that looks good at 6 months may partially resorb by 2-3 years, creating unpredictable late deterioration

nodes.label: CT scanning and CAD-CAM skull modelling

properties.step: First step of the procedure - precedes implant manufacturing
properties.output: Precise anatomical model of patient's skull or 3D simulated computer image
properties.mirror_technique: For unilateral defects, unaffected side is mirrored to create reconstruction template
properties.implant_fabrication: Implant shaped against contours of the model
properties.computer_assisted_navigation: Can be used intraoperatively to assess accuracy of positioning
properties.tacit: The quality of the CT data and the accuracy of the skull model are fundamental to the outcome. Inadequate resolution or motion artefact propagates error into implant design. The clinician must critically evaluate image quality before proceeding to manufacture.

nodes.label: Soft tissue flap for implant coverage

properties.purpose: Provides vascularised soft tissue cover over titanium implant
properties.relevance: Critical determinant of outcome - adequate coverage prevents exposure, infection, and implant failure
properties.failure_risk: Flap necrosis (4.2% in radiotherapy patients in Sun J 2011)
properties.tacit: Soft tissue coverage is the key variable that separates HTG307 from HTG313. Coverage quality must be assessed preoperatively and intraoperatively. Radiotherapy significantly increases the risk of flap failure and coverage breakdown.

properties.type: Free flap combining bone, muscle, and skin from fibula
properties.use: Maxillary reconstruction - palatal soft tissue restoration with bone
properties.combination_with_titanium: Used with titanium mesh or zygomatic implant for structural support
properties.necrosis_risk: 4.2% flap loss in Sun J case series - associated with prior radiotherapy
properties.osteoradionecrosis_risk: 5% partial fibular osteoradionecrosis in Sun J series

properties.relevance: Most common indication for orofacial reconstruction
properties.tacit: Timing of reconstruction significantly affects outcomes. Tang W 2010 showed better diplopia resolution for fresh fractures (67%) vs old fractures (30%). Early reconstruction before significant scarring and fibrosis develops improves functional outcomes.

properties.relevance: Major indication for orofacial reconstruction with titanium implants
properties.radiotherapy_factor: Many patients have received or will receive radiotherapy, which significantly increases complication risk
properties.sun_j_2011: 45% immediate reconstruction, 55% secondary in case series of high maxillectomy defects
properties.tacit: The radiotherapy status is a critical pre-operative assessment. Sun J 2011 showed ALL complications (flap loss, osteoradionecrosis, mesh exposure, fistulae) occurred in radiotherapy patients. Non-irradiated fields have a fundamentally different risk profile.

properties.relevance: Less common but recognised indication for customised titanium implant
properties.note: Less represented in evidence base than trauma and tumour reconstruction

properties.mechanism: Direct or indirect force causing wall fracture with or without displacement
properties.timing_significance: Old fractures (>2 weeks) have worse diplopia outcomes due to established scarring and fibrosis

properties.definition: Brown class 3 defect - resection includes orbital floor with orbital contents preserved
properties.reconstruction_requirement: Requires structural support and soft tissue restoration
properties.components: Titanium mesh for structure + osteomyocutaneous flap for soft tissue/bone

properties.definition: Posterior displacement of the eye globe within the orbit
properties.measurement: Hertel exophthalmometry; ≥2mm difference between eyes = clinically significant
properties.mechanism: Increased orbital volume without corresponding increase in orbital contents volume
properties.cause_in_context: Typically from orbital wall fracture expanding orbital cavity
properties.overcorrection_risk: 10.3% (3/29) in Lieger 2010 - classified as 'resolved' but technically still abnormal
properties.tacit: Overcorrection is distinct from correction failure. A 10% overcorrection rate means the surgeon must plan for slight undercorrection to account for post-operative swelling resolution and soft tissue settling. Aim for 1-2mm overcorrection acutely.

properties.definition: Double vision at one or more positions of gaze
properties.mechanism: Extraocular muscle entrapment, orbital soft tissue disruption, or nerve injury
properties.measurement: Binocular single vision screen (% of visual field with double images); prism dioptres
properties.tacit: Diplopia is the most stubborn symptom to resolve. Complete resolution is less likely than for enophthalmos. Old fractures with established scarring have significantly worse diplopia outcomes. Intraconal scarring may cause persistent diplopia that does not respond to implant position correction alone.

properties.definition: Reduced range of eye movement, typically from muscle entrapment or fibrosis
properties.cause_in_context: Orbital fracture with muscle entrapment or intraconal scarring
properties.tacit: Ocular motility is the least likely to fully resolve. 50% of affected patients in Lieger 2010 had no change. Severe intraconal scarring (posterior to equator of globe) is particularly refractory - the globe cannot project without inferior rotation and revisional surgery has limited benefit.

properties.measurement: Clinical assessment; CT-based zygoma reduction accuracy
properties.tacit: Facial asymmetry is the most consistently resolved outcome with customised implants. The benefit of customisation over traditional methods is most pronounced for facial symmetry outcomes.

properties.cause_in_context: High maxillectomy defects disrupt palate and oral cavity geometry
properties.resolution_rate: Intelligible speech in 95% (19/20) of high maxillectomy patients at 6 months (Sun J 2011)

properties.cause_in_context: Maxillary defect disrupts dental arch and occlusal contacts
properties.resolution: All patients tolerated regular or soft diet after reconstruction
properties.occlusal_force: Mean postoperative occlusal force 61.4±10.3% of preoperative (Sun J 2011)
properties.dental_rehabilitation: 50% had delayed dental rehabilitation due to economic reasons or patient refusal

properties.surface_interaction: When covered with soft tissue, fibrous ingrowth and epithelialisation occurs over time
properties.exposure_risk: If soft tissue breaks down, mesh may become exposed - particularly in irradiated fields
properties.tacit: Mesh thickness selection is an expert decision balancing structural support vs. profile/palpability. Thinner mesh (0.25mm) is less palpable but provides less rigidity. In areas with thin overlying tissue (orbital floor), palpability complaints are more common.

properties.purpose: Fixation of implant to adjacent bone
properties.complication: Palpable screw heads can cause discomfort, particularly at orbital margin
properties.management: Screw removal or plate trimming if symptomatic (1/22 patients, Mustafa 2011)

properties.use: Facial reconstruction - alternative to or combination with titanium mesh
properties.composite_use: Titanium mesh embedded in porous polyethylene
properties.advantage: Allows fibrovascular ingrowth; lower profile than titanium alone
properties.used_in: He D 2012 study (18.8% Medpor only, 15.6% titanium + Medpor)

properties.type: Calcium phosphate ceramic - mimics bone mineral
properties.used_in: He D 2012 study (7.8% hydroxyapatite only, 3.1% titanium + hydroxyapatite)

properties.definition: Titanium mesh becomes unintentionally exposed through overlying soft tissue
properties.incidence: 5.3% (1/19) oral cavity at 36 months; 5.3% (1/19) infraorbital skin at 4 months (Sun J 2011)
properties.management: Further surgery to remove exposed mesh and cover with flap
properties.detection: Can occur months to years post-operatively - requires long-term surveillance
properties.tacit: Implant exposure in irradiated tissue is a late complication that requires active surveillance. Early signs include persistent wound erythema, breakdown of overlying tissue, or palpable change. Delay in recognition leads to increased extent of required revision.

properties.outcome: Both resolved without implant removal in reported cases
properties.theoretical_risk: Life-threatening sepsis noted by specialist advisers as theoretical risk
properties.tacit: Infection risk is amplified by: prior radiotherapy, proximity to oral cavity, inadequate flap vascularity, immunosuppression. The distinction between superficial wound infection (antibiotics) and deep peri-implant sepsis (may require implant removal) is a critical clinical decision.

properties.incidence_serious: 4.5% (1/22) sudden onset complete visual loss - resolved spontaneously after 1 week; 7% (1/15) transient visual acuity loss from optic nerve encroachment

mechanism.sudden_onset: Unknown mechanism; plate removed 24 hours post-procedure; spontaneous recovery over 1 week
mechanism.optic_nerve_encroachment: Mesh plate encroached on optic nerve; reoperation led to complete recovery

properties.urgency: URGENT - optic nerve encroachment requires immediate reoperation
properties.tacit: This is the highest-stakes immediate complication. Post-operative visual monitoring is essential. Any sudden visual change post-surgery = optic nerve compromise until proven otherwise. CT/imaging and ophthalmological assessment must be immediate.

properties.mechanism: Surgical injury or pressure to oculomotor nerve during orbital reconstruction

properties.definition: Scarring of soft tissues posterior to the equator of the eye globe, within the cone of extraocular muscles
properties.consequence: Globe cannot project without significant inferior rotation; persistent diplopia and limited motility
properties.management: Revisional surgery for extraocular muscle entrapment (diplopia improved)
properties.tacit: Intraconal scarring is distinct from orbital wall failure. The implant may be perfectly positioned, but pre-existing intraconal scar tissue from prior injury prevents normal globe projection. This is why Bell 2009 noted that 'suboptimal enophthalmos correction' was not considered a complication when due to scarring.

types: Lower lid ectropion (4.7% in He D 2012 n=64)
Lower lid retraction (7% in Bell 2009 n=15)

management.ectropion: Often resolves spontaneously; massage; occasional further surgery

properties.mechanism: Disruption of lower eyelid support structures during orbital reconstruction

properties.incidence: 10.5% (2/19) in Sun J 2011 maxillary reconstruction series
properties.mechanism: Failure of palatal soft tissue paddle at margins, particularly in irradiated tissue

types: Complete flap loss (4.2%, 1/24 flaps in Sun J 2011 - vascular compromise)
Partial fibular osteoradionecrosis (5%, 1/20 in Sun J 2011)

management.complete_loss: Necrotic portion removed; titanium mesh preserved; further procedure

properties.tacit: Radiotherapy causes endothelial damage and impairs vascular ingrowth, fundamentally compromising flap survival. Pre-operative assessment of radiation dose, field, and timing since treatment is essential risk stratification.

properties.note: Treatment group (customised vs traditional) not recorded

nodes.label: Palpable plate rim and screw discomfort

properties.symptoms: Pain and irritation at inferior orbital margin; patient can palpate plate flange and screw head
properties.management: Trimming of plate flange and screw removal in further operation

properties.classification: Classified as 'resolved' in that study despite slight overcorrection
properties.definition: Globe projects further anteriorly than normal side
properties.tacit: Overcorrection is an under-recognised complication because it may be classified as 'success'. The clinician must aim for slight undercorrection to account for post-operative oedema resolution, which will make the correction appear less severe over time. Planned undercorrection by 1-2mm is standard expert practice.

properties.note: 3 of 4 patients had mild pre-operative atrophy that worsened
properties.cause: Disruption of temporalis muscle or blood supply during surgical approach

properties.mechanism: Bone resorption, infection, inadequate initial fixation
properties.consequence: Loss of reconstruction, symptomatic implant, re-operation required

properties.authors: He D, Li Z, Shi W et al.
properties.title: Orbitozygomatic fractures with enophthalmos: analysis of 64 cases treated late
properties.journal: Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery

properties.authors: Kozakiewicz M, Elgalal M, Piotr L et al.
properties.title: Treatment with individual orbital wall implants in humans – 1-year ophthalmologic evaluation
properties.population: Orbital fractures without CNS or globe injury
properties.key_finding: Customised significantly better binocular vision (p=0.015), upgaze (p=0.003), globe position (p=0.012)

properties.authors: Andrades P, Hernandez D, Falguera MI et al.
properties.title: Degrees of tolerance in post-traumatic orbital volume correction: the role of prefabricated mesh
properties.journal: Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
properties.key_finding: Prefabricated mesh significantly better orbital volume (p=0.011), apex-globe distance (p=0.007), orbital rim area (p=0.023)

properties.authors: Lieger O, Richards R, Liu M et al.
properties.title: Computer-assisted design and manufacture of implants in the late reconstruction of extensive orbital fractures
properties.population: Post-traumatic enophthalmos ≥2mm with or without diplopia

properties.authors: Tang W, Guo L, Long J et al.
properties.title: Individual design and rapid prototyping in reconstruction of orbital wall defects
properties.journal: Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
properties.key_finding: Fresh fractures have better diplopia resolution (67%) than old fractures (30%)

properties.authors: Mustafa SF, Evans PL, Bocca A et al.
properties.title: Customized titanium reconstruction of post-traumatic orbital wall defects: a review of 22 cases
properties.journal: International Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery

properties.authors: Sun J, Shen Y, Li J et al.
properties.title: Reconstruction of high maxillectomy defects with the fibula osteomyocutaneous flap in combination with titanium mesh or a zygomatic implant
properties.key_finding: All complications occurred in radiotherapy patients

properties.title: Computer-assisted planning, stereolithographic modeling, and intraoperative navigation for complex orbital reconstruction
properties.journal: Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
properties.population: Complex primary or secondary post-traumatic and post-ablative orbital deformities
properties.technique: Titanium mesh with computer-assisted intraoperative navigation
properties.follow_up: Not reported beyond initial postoperative period

properties.document_type: NICE HealthTech guidance (formerly Interventional Procedures Guidance)
specialist_societies: British Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons
Craniofacial Society of Great Britain and Ireland

nodes.label: Customised implant superiority over traditional method

advantages: Improved safety in limited visibility areas (e.g. around optic nerve)
Better functional and cosmetic outcomes (statistically demonstrated)
properties.tacit: The benefit is largest for functional visual outcomes. The customised approach's advantage around the optic nerve (where re-positioning attempts are especially hazardous) is the key safety argument for customisation in orbital surgery.

properties.effect_on_outcomes: He 2012: 100% perfect zygoma reduction with navigation vs 85.7% without; 90.9% good globe projection vs 64.3%
properties.mechanism: Real-time verification of implant position against pre-operative plan
properties.use_case: Complex anatomy, limited visibility, revision surgery, orbital reconstruction near optic nerve
properties.tacit: Navigation provides objective, real-time feedback that compensates for the limits of haptic and visual assessment alone. In areas where even 1-2mm mispositioning has significant consequences (orbital floor near optic nerve), navigation moves the procedure from 'expert estimation' to 'verified positioning'.

properties.significance: All complications in Sun J 2011 occurred in radiotherapy patients
properties.mechanism: Radiation causes endothelial damage, impairs vascularisation and wound healing, increases infection risk, causes osteoradionecrosis
properties.pre_operative_assessment: Must assess radiation dose, field, and time since treatment
properties.tacit: Radiotherapy history is a fundamental risk stratification factor that changes the expected complication profile. A non-irradiated patient and a 60Gy-irradiated patient having identical procedures have fundamentally different risk profiles. Clinicians must actively seek radiation history.

properties.definition: Fresh = within 2 weeks; old = more than 2 weeks
properties.effect_on_diplopia: Fresh fractures: 67% diplopia resolution; old fractures: 30% (Tang W 2010)
properties.mechanism: Old fractures develop fibrosis, muscle atrophy, and intraconal scarring that impairs globe mobility
properties.tacit: The 2-week threshold is not absolute. The key message is that intraconal scarring and muscle fibrosis progressively limit the achievable functional outcome. Earlier intervention generally gives better diplopia outcomes. However, significant oedema in the immediate post-injury period can make early reconstruction technically difficult.

properties.description: Whether adequate soft tissue coverage can be achieved or is expected to form
properties.determines_guidance_pathway: Coverage achievable → HTG307 (normal arrangements); Coverage not achievable → HTG313 (special arrangements)
properties.exceptions: Paranasal sinus area - partial exposure acceptable as epithelialisation expected
properties.tacit: This is the single most important clinical decision in orofacial titanium reconstruction. The clinician must assess coverage preoperatively based on local tissue availability, flap options, and tissue quality (especially in irradiated fields). The boundary between HTG307 and HTG313 is not always clinically obvious - a technically covered implant with poor-quality tissue may fail to maintain coverage.

properties.contrast_with: HTG20 and HTG313 which require special arrangements
properties.tacit: Normal arrangements means the procedure can be offered without special institutional notification, but clinician training requirements, specialist unit designation, and audit commitments still apply as standard NHS governance

properties.requirement: CAD-CAM techniques taught in large specialist centres
properties.adviser_comment: Adoption into mainstream practice considered 'extremely unlikely' by specialist advisers
properties.clinical_implication: This is effectively a de facto specialist centre restriction, even though NICE guidance says 'normal arrangements'
properties.tacit: The NICE recommendation says 'normal arrangements', but the specialist advisers flag that CAD-CAM manufacturing technology requires skilled computer and manufacturing expertise, and costly equipment. The practical effect is that only specialist centres will have the infrastructure to offer this procedure.

edges.label: Orofacial trauma is the most common indication for customised titanium implant reconstruction
Orofacial tumour removal creates reconstruction defects requiring titanium implant
Congenital facial abnormality may indicate customised titanium reconstruction
Orbital floor fracture causes enophthalmos by expanding orbital volume
Orbital floor fracture causes diplopia via muscle entrapment and disruption
Orbital floor fracture causes restricted ocular motility via extraocular muscle effects
Orofacial trauma causes facial asymmetry from displaced or comminuted fractures
High maxillary defect causes speech impairment from palate and oral cavity disruption
High maxillary defect causes masticatory dysfunction from dental arch disruption
CT scanning and CAD-CAM modelling is the first step of the procedure

properties.sequence: Step 1 - mandatory precursor to implant manufacture

edges.label: CAD-CAM model is used to shape the customised titanium mesh

properties.method: Implant shaped against contours of the anatomical model

edges.label: Procedure uses customised titanium mesh as primary structural material
Titanium screws fix implant to adjacent bone
Procedure requires soft tissue coverage of implant in most cases

properties.exception: Paranasal sinus area - partial exposure acceptable

edges.label: Soft tissue coverage options include fibula osteomyocutaneous flap for maxillary reconstruction
Customised implant is superior to traditional intraoperative bending

properties.outcome_data: Statistically superior on multiple visual and facial outcomes

edges.label: Customised titanium implant is an alternative to autologous bone graft

properties.advantage_over_graft: No donor site morbidity; no resorption; predictable long-term outcomes
properties.disadvantage_vs_graft: Implant not biological; theoretical infection and exposure risks

edges.label: Computer-assisted navigation augments implant placement accuracy

properties.evidence: He 2012: 100% perfect zygoma reduction with navigation vs 85.7% without
properties.indication: Complex anatomy, near optic nerve, orbital reconstruction
properties.tacit_note: Navigation is recommended when margin for error is small - particularly orbital floor near optic nerve

edges.label: Procedure treats enophthalmos by restoring orbital volume and wall architecture
Procedure treats diplopia by correcting extraocular muscle position

properties.resolution_rates: resolved 21-67%; improvement common; incomplete resolution in some
properties.tacit_note: Diplopia outcomes are highly variable and depend on fracture age, degree of scarring, and patient anatomy

edges.label: Procedure partially treats restricted ocular motility

properties.tacit_note: Motility is least likely to fully resolve; intraconal scarring is refractory to structural correction

edges.label: Procedure most reliably resolves facial asymmetry

properties.resolution_rates: 100% for malar depression/prominence (Bell 2009); 85.7-100% perfect zygoma reduction (He 2012)
properties.tacit_note: Facial asymmetry has the best and most consistent response to customised implants

edges.label: Procedure treats speech impairment in maxillary reconstruction
Procedure risks titanium mesh exposure if soft tissue coverage fails

properties.incidence: 5.3% per site in irradiated patients (Sun J 2011)
properties.timing: Can occur months to years post-operatively

edges.label: Procedure risks peri-implant infection
Procedure risks visual deterioration including optic nerve injury

properties.urgency: Optic nerve encroachment requires immediate reoperation

edges.label: Procedure risks oculomotor nerve palsy
Procedure or underlying injury may lead to intraconal scarring with persistent diplopia

properties.note: May be pre-existing from injury; compounded by surgical manipulation

edges.label: Procedure risks eyelid complications including ectropion and scleral show
Maxillary reconstruction risks oronasal fistula
Maxillary reconstruction with fibula flap risks flap necrosis
Procedure risks postoperative haematoma
Procedure risks palpable plate rim causing discomfort
Procedure risks overcorrection of enophthalmos

properties.tacit_note: Expert practice is to plan slight undercorrection

edges.label: Procedure carries theoretical risk of bone resorption
Procedure carries theoretical risk of implant loosening
Prior radiotherapy significantly amplifies risk of implant exposure

properties.evidence: All mesh exposure cases in Sun J 2011 were in irradiated patients

edges.label: Prior radiotherapy significantly amplifies risk of flap necrosis

properties.evidence: All flap complications in Sun J 2011 were in irradiated patients

edges.label: Prior radiotherapy amplifies oronasal fistula risk

properties.evidence: All oronasal fistulae in Sun J 2011 were in irradiated patients

edges.label: Fracture timing predicts diplopia resolution rate

properties.mechanism: Established intraconal scarring in old fractures impairs muscle mobility

edges.label: Soft tissue coverage adequacy determines whether HTG307 or HTG313 applies

properties.gate_logic: Coverage achievable → HTG307 (normal arrangements); Coverage not achievable → HTG313 (special arrangements)
properties.tacit_note: This is the key branching decision. Coverage adequacy must be assessed pre-operatively, not assumed.

edges.label: Implant exposure requires revision surgery to remove exposed mesh and cover with flap
Peri-implant infection may lead to implant exposure if untreated

properties.tacit_note: Infection weakens soft tissue coverage, creating a pathway to exposure

edges.label: Visual deterioration from optic nerve encroachment triggers urgent reoperation
Intraconal scarring causes persistent diplopia refractory to structural correction

properties.management: Revisional surgery for extraocular muscle entrapment - diplopia improved

edges.label: He 2012 demonstrates customised implant superiority for globe projection and zygoma reduction
Kozakiewicz 2011 demonstrates statistically significant superiority for diplopia and globe position
Andrades 2009 demonstrates prefabricated mesh superiority for orbital volume correction
He 2012 demonstrates further improvement with navigation: 90.9% globe projection, 100% zygoma reduction
Tang W 2010 establishes that fresh fractures have better diplopia outcomes than old fractures
Sun J 2011 establishes that all major complications occurred in irradiated patients
NICE HTG307 requires normal (not special) governance arrangements

properties.contrast: HTG20 and HTG313 require special arrangements

edges.label: CAD-CAM technique requirements effectively limit procedure to specialist centres

properties.source: Specialist advisers comments in NICE overview
properties.tacit_note: The 'normal arrangements' recommendation does not mean 'any hospital can do this'. CAD-CAM manufacturing infrastructure exists only in specialist centres.

edges.label: Customised implants effectively supersede traditional intraoperative bending where available
Autologous grafts were the historical standard before customised titanium became available

properties.limitation: Variable resorption was key limitation of autologous approach

edges.label: Orbital floor fracture is a subtype of orofacial trauma
High maxillary defect typically results from tumour ablation
Fibula flap risks necrosis particularly in irradiated patients
Adequate soft tissue coverage protects against peri-implant infection

properties.mechanism: Vascularised tissue brings immune cells and seals implant from oral/external flora

edges.label: Surgical approach risks temporal region atrophy from temporalis muscle disruption
Overcorrection is the inverse failure mode to under-correction of enophthalmos

properties.tacit_note: Success requires landing in a narrow range. The surgeon must actively plan for post-operative swelling resolution when selecting implant dimensions.

edges.label: Lieger 2010 documents 10.3% overcorrection rate
Mustafa 2011 documents sudden visual deterioration with spontaneous recovery
Bell 2009 documents transient visual acuity loss from optic nerve plate encroachment
Bell 2009 documents intraconal scarring with persistent diplopia requiring revisional surgery

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Coverage adequacy as the primary branching decision
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: The single most critical pre-operative assessment is whether adequate soft tissue coverage can be achieved or expected. This decision determines the entire governance pathway (HTG307 vs HTG313) and fundamentally changes the risk profile. A technically challenging coverage is not the same as adequate coverage - especially in irradiated fields.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Radiotherapy as a universal risk multiplier
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Prior radiotherapy changes the entire post-operative risk profile. Sun J 2011 showed that ALL major complications (flap necrosis, mesh exposure, osteoradionecrosis, oronasal fistula) occurred exclusively in irradiated patients. This history must be elicited as part of pre-operative assessment and should trigger higher-level surveillance and lower threshold for intervention.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Navigation when optic nerve is at risk
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Computer-assisted intraoperative navigation should be used when implant placement is near the optic nerve. He 2012 demonstrated 100% perfect zygoma reduction with navigation vs 85.7% without. Transient visual loss from optic nerve encroachment (Bell 2009) highlights the consequence of 1-2mm mispositioning. Navigation moves from 'expert estimation' to 'verified positioning' in high-stakes anatomy.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Fracture timing and functional outcome expectations
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Diplopia outcomes depend critically on fracture age. Old fractures (>2 weeks) have established intraconal scarring that impairs muscle function independent of implant position. The clinician must counsel patients that even perfect orbital volume restoration may not resolve diplopia if scarring has developed. Setting outcome expectations before surgery is part of the consultation.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Overcorrection planning
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: The 10.3% overcorrection rate in Lieger 2010 arises because surgeons aim for correction at the time of implant placement, not accounting for post-operative oedema resolution. Expert practice plans for 1-2mm undercorrection acutely, knowing that as oedema resolves, the apparent correction will decrease. This is not documented in guidance text but is essential tacit knowledge.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: CAD-CAM infrastructure as de facto specialist centre restriction
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Despite NICE recommending 'normal arrangements', the CAD-CAM manufacturing infrastructure required is only available at specialist centres. Specialist advisers noted adoption into mainstream practice is 'extremely unlikely'. The practical governance effect is specialist centre restriction despite the guidance wording suggesting routine use.
